---
title: "UI_P011: Hierarchical Navigation Principle"
subtitle: "The UI should implement a clear hierarchy of navigation elements, with primary navigation at the top level and consistent secondary navigation within each primary section. This hierarchical approach makes the application's structure intuitive and provides clear context for user interactions"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "principle"
number: "UI_P011"
date-created: "2025-04-09"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "UI Components Law"
article: 
  number: 11
  title: "Hierarchical Navigation Principle"
related_to:
  - "MP017: Modularity Principle"
  - "MP067: UI Separation Meta Principle"
  - "R092: bs4Dash Navbar Navigation Rule"
  - "R011: Hybrid Sidebar Pattern"
  - "UI_P009: Single-Line UI Elements Principle"
  - "UI_P010: Minimal CSS Usage Principle"
  - "R014: UI Hierarchy Rule"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 11: Hierarchical Navigation Principle {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 11 (Hierarchical Navigation Principle)**

The UI should implement a clear hierarchy of navigation elements, with primary navigation at the top level and consistent secondary navigation within each primary section. This hierarchical approach makes the application's structure intuitive and provides clear context for user interactions.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Create an intuitive user experience that helps users navigate effectively through the application by clearly defining and separating top-level and second-level navigation.
:::

# Section 1: Implementation Requirements {#implementation-requirements}

## Subsection 1: Top-Level Navigation {#top-level-navigation}

**Top-Level Navigation Standards:**

- Use bs4Dash navbarMenu and navbarTab components for primary navigation (following R92)
- Place at the top of the interface in a consistent location
- Clearly distinguish active vs. inactive states
- Focus on major application sections/modules

**Implementation Example:**

```r
# Top-level navigation in UI definition
header = bs4Dash::dashboardHeader(
  navbarMenu(
    id = "main_navbar",
    navbarTab(
      text = HTML('<span class="navbarTab-content">
        <i class="fa fa-user mr-1"></i>
        <span>Micro Analysis</span>
      </span>'),
      tabName = "micro_tab"
    ),
    navbarTab(
      text = HTML('<span class="navbarTab-content">
        <i class="fa fa-chart-bar mr-1"></i>
        <span>Macro Analysis</span>
      </span>'),
      tabName = "macro_tab"
    )
  )
)
```

## Subsection 2: Second-Level Navigation {#second-level-navigation}

**Second-Level Navigation Standards:**

- Implement using action buttons at the top of each primary section's content
- Apply consistent styling for all second-level navigation elements
- Ensure buttons have both icon and text for improved usability
- Place in a container with proper spacing and alignment
- Use active/inactive visual styling to indicate current selection

**Implementation Example:**

```r
# Second-level navigation in UI definition
div(
  class = "second-level-nav-container",
  bs4Dash::box(
    width = 12,
    status = "primary",
    solidHeader = FALSE,
    uiOutput("micro_subtabs")
  )
)

# In server function
micro_active_subtab <- reactiveVal("customer")

output$micro_subtabs <- renderUI({
  div(
    class = "d-flex justify-content-center my-2",
    actionButton(
      inputId = "micro_subtab_customer",
      label = translate("Customer Profile"),
      icon = icon("user"),
      class = paste0("btn ", 
        if(micro_active_subtab() == "customer") "btn-primary" else "btn-default"),
      style = "margin: 5px; min-width: 120px;"
    ),
    actionButton(
      inputId = "micro_subtab_dna",
      label = translate("DNA Distribution"),
      icon = icon("dna"),
      class = paste0("btn ", 
        if(micro_active_subtab() == "dna") "btn-primary" else "btn-default"),
      style = "margin: 5px; min-width: 120px;"
    )
  )
})
```

# Section 2: Navigation State Management {#navigation-state-management}

## Subsection 1: State Tracking Mechanisms {#state-tracking}

**State Management Requirements:**

- Track active state for each level of navigation independently
- Use reactive values for managing the active state
- Implement proper event handlers to update navigation state
- Ensure content visibility toggles correctly with navigation changes

**Implementation Example:**

```r
# Navigation state manager
createNavigationManager <- function() {
  # Initialize states
  states <- reactiveValues(
    primary = "micro_tab",
    micro_secondary = "customer",
    macro_secondary = "trends"
  )
  
  list(
    # Get current state
    getState = function(level = NULL) {
      if (is.null(level)) {
        return(reactiveValuesToList(states))
      }
      return(states[[level]])
    },
    
    # Set state
    setState = function(level, value) {
      states[[level]] <- value
    },
    
    # Get active path
    getActivePath = function() {
      c(states$primary, 
        switch(states$primary,
          micro_tab = states$micro_secondary,
          macro_tab = states$macro_secondary
        ))
    },
    
    # Reset state
    reset = function() {
      states$primary <- "micro_tab"
      states$micro_secondary <- "customer"
      states$macro_secondary <- "trends"
    }
  )
}

# Use navigation manager
nav_manager <- createNavigationManager()
```

## Subsection 2: Event Handling {#event-handling}

**Navigation Event Handling:**

```r
# Handle clicks on micro subtabs
observeEvent(input$micro_subtab_customer, {
  # Update navigation state
  micro_active_subtab("customer")
  nav_manager$setState("micro_secondary", "customer")
  
  # Toggle component visibility
  if (!is.null(micro_components_union) && 
      !is.null(micro_components_union$component_state) && 
      is.function(micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component)) {
    
    # Show customer component
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("customer", TRUE)
    # Hide other components
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("dna", FALSE)
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("analysis", FALSE)
  }
  
  # Log navigation action
  logNavigation("micro", "customer", session$user)
})

observeEvent(input$micro_subtab_dna, {
  micro_active_subtab("dna")
  nav_manager$setState("micro_secondary", "dna")
  
  if (!is.null(micro_components_union)) {
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("customer", FALSE)
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("dna", TRUE)
    micro_components_union$component_state$toggle_component("analysis", FALSE)
  }
  
  logNavigation("micro", "dna", session$user)
})
```

# Section 3: Visual Consistency {#visual-consistency}

## Subsection 1: Styling Standards {#styling-standards}

**Visual Consistency Requirements:**

- Maintain consistent height and alignment of all navigation elements
- Follow UI_P009: Single-Line UI Elements Principle for button layout
- Use minimal CSS following UI_P010: Minimal CSS Usage Principle
- Apply proper spacing between navigation levels
- Ensure clear visual hierarchy between primary and secondary navigation

**CSS Implementation:**

```css
/* Top-level navigation styles */
.navbarTab-content {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  height: 40px;
  line-height: 40px;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.navbarTab-content i {
  margin-right: 5px;
  display: inline-flex;
}

/* Second-level navigation container */
.second-level-nav-container {
  background: #f4f4f4;
  padding: 10px 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

/* Navigation button styles */
.second-level-nav-container .btn {
  height: 36px;
  line-height: 36px;
  padding: 0 15px;
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.second-level-nav-container .btn i {
  margin-right: 5px;
}

/* Active state styles */
.btn-primary {
  background-color: #007bff;
  border-color: #007bff;
  color: white;
}

.btn-default {
  background-color: white;
  border-color: #dee2e6;
  color: #333;
}

.btn-default:hover {
  background-color: #e9ecef;
}
```

## Subsection 2: Responsive Design {#responsive-design}

**Responsive Navigation Implementation:**

```r
# Responsive second-level navigation
output$responsive_subtabs <- renderUI({
  screen_width <- input$screen_width %||% 1920
  
  if (screen_width < 768) {
    # Mobile: dropdown menu
    selectInput(
      inputId = "micro_subtab_select",
      label = NULL,
      choices = list(
        "Customer Profile" = "customer",
        "DNA Distribution" = "dna",
        "Behavior Analysis" = "analysis"
      ),
      selected = micro_active_subtab()
    )
  } else {
    # Desktop: button group
    div(
      class = "d-flex justify-content-center my-2",
      lapply(list(
        list(id = "customer", label = "Customer Profile", icon = "user"),
        list(id = "dna", label = "DNA Distribution", icon = "dna"),
        list(id = "analysis", label = "Behavior Analysis", icon = "chart-line")
      ), function(btn) {
        actionButton(
          inputId = paste0("micro_subtab_", btn$id),
          label = translate(btn$label),
          icon = icon(btn$icon),
          class = paste0("btn ", 
            if(micro_active_subtab() == btn$id) "btn-primary" else "btn-default"),
          style = "margin: 5px; min-width: 120px;"
        )
      })
    )
  }
})

# Handle responsive selection
observeEvent(input$micro_subtab_select, {
  micro_active_subtab(input$micro_subtab_select)
  updateNavigationDisplay(input$micro_subtab_select)
})
```

# Section 4: Benefits Analysis {#benefits-analysis}

## Subsection 1: User Experience Improvements {#ux-improvements}

**User Experience Advantages:**

1. **Intuitive Navigation Flow**: Navigation flow from general to specific
2. **Clear Position Indicators**: Visual indicators of current location in the application
3. **Reduced Cognitive Load**: Reduced user thinking through consistent patterns
4. **Quick Access**: Hierarchical structure allows quick navigation to specific features
5. **Context Preservation**: Maintains user work context during navigation

## Subsection 2: Maintenance and Performance Advantages {#maintenance-performance}

**Technical Advantages:**

1. **Maintenance Benefits**:
   - Standardized approach for implementing navigation
   - Clearer code organization separating navigation levels
   - Easier to extend with additional sections or sub-sections

2. **Performance Advantages**:
   - Reduced server load by toggling visibility rather than rebuilding UI
   - Minimized UI redraws when switching between secondary options
   - Better responsiveness for navigation interactions

# Section 5: Complete Implementation Example {#complete-implementation}

## Subsection 1: Complete Navigation System {#complete-navigation-system}

**Application Implementation:**

```r
# navigation_system.R - Complete hierarchical navigation system
library(shiny)
library(bs4Dash)

# Navigation configuration
nav_config <- list(
  primary = list(
    micro = list(
      label = "Micro Analysis",
      icon = "user",
      subtabs = list(
        customer = list(label = "Customer Profile", icon = "user"),
        dna = list(label = "DNA Distribution", icon = "dna"),
        behavior = list(label = "Behavior Analysis", icon = "chart-line")
      )
    ),
    macro = list(
      label = "Macro Analysis",
      icon = "chart-bar",
      subtabs = list(
        trends = list(label = "Trend Analysis", icon = "trending-up"),
        segments = list(label = "Market Segments", icon = "pie-chart"),
        forecast = list(label = "Forecast Model", icon = "crystal-ball")
      )
    ),
    settings = list(
      label = "Settings",
      icon = "cog",
      subtabs = list(
        profile = list(label = "Profile", icon = "id-card"),
        preferences = list(label = "Preferences", icon = "sliders-h"),
        security = list(label = "Security", icon = "shield-alt")
      )
    )
  )
)

ui <- bs4DashPage(
  title = "Hierarchical Navigation Demo",
  
  # Top-level navigation
  header = bs4DashNavbar(
    title = "Application",
    navbarMenu(
      id = "main_navbar",
      lapply(names(nav_config$primary), function(tab_id) {
        tab_info <- nav_config$primary[[tab_id]]
        navbarTab(
          text = HTML(sprintf(
            '<span class="navbarTab-content">
              <i class="fa fa-%s mr-1"></i>
              <span>%s</span>
            </span>',
            tab_info$icon, tab_info$label
          )),
          tabName = paste0(tab_id, "_tab")
        )
      })
    )
  ),
  
  sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(
    disable = TRUE  # Use hierarchical navigation instead of sidebar
  ),
  
  body = bs4DashBody(
    # Navigation styles
    tags$style(HTML("
      .navbarTab-content {
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        height: 40px;
      }
      .second-level-nav-container {
        background: #f8f9fa;
        padding: 10px 0;
        border-bottom: 2px solid #dee2e6;
      }
      .nav-btn-active {
        background: #007bff !important;
        color: white !important;
      }
    ")),
    
    # Content area
    uiOutput("navigation_content")
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Navigation state management
  nav_state <- reactiveValues(
    primary = "micro",
    secondary = list(
      micro = "customer",
      macro = "trends",
      settings = "profile"
    )
  )
  
  # Primary navigation observer
  observeEvent(input$main_navbar, {
    tab_name <- gsub("_tab$", "", input$main_navbar)
    nav_state$primary <- tab_name
  })
  
  # Render navigation content
  output$navigation_content <- renderUI({
    req(nav_state$primary)
    
    primary_tab <- nav_state$primary
    tab_config <- nav_config$primary[[primary_tab]]
    
    tagList(
      # Second-level navigation
      div(
        class = "second-level-nav-container",
        div(
          class = "container-fluid",
          div(
            class = "d-flex justify-content-center",
            lapply(names(tab_config$subtabs), function(subtab_id) {
              subtab_info <- tab_config$subtabs[[subtab_id]]
              is_active <- nav_state$secondary[[primary_tab]] == subtab_id
              
              actionButton(
                inputId = paste0(primary_tab, "_", subtab_id, "_btn"),
                label = subtab_info$label,
                icon = icon(subtab_info$icon),
                class = paste("btn", if(is_active) "nav-btn-active" else "btn-outline-secondary"),
                style = "margin: 0 5px;"
              )
            })
          )
        )
      ),
      
      # Content area
      div(
        class = "container-fluid mt-3",
        uiOutput(paste0(primary_tab, "_content"))
      )
    )
  })
  
  # Dynamically create second-level navigation observers
  observe({
    req(nav_state$primary)
    
    primary_tab <- nav_state$primary
    tab_config <- nav_config$primary[[primary_tab]]
    
    lapply(names(tab_config$subtabs), function(subtab_id) {
      btn_id <- paste0(primary_tab, "_", subtab_id, "_btn")
      
      observeEvent(input[[btn_id]], {
        nav_state$secondary[[primary_tab]] <- subtab_id
        
        # Log navigation action
        message(sprintf("Navigated to: %s > %s", 
          tab_config$label, 
          tab_config$subtabs[[subtab_id]]$label))
      })
    })
  })
  
  # Micro analysis content
  output$micro_content <- renderUI({
    req(nav_state$secondary$micro)
    
    content <- switch(nav_state$secondary$micro,
      customer = bs4Card(
        title = "Customer Profile",
        status = "primary",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Customer profile content...")
      ),
      dna = bs4Card(
        title = "DNA Distribution",
        status = "info",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("DNA distribution analysis content...")
      ),
      behavior = bs4Card(
        title = "Behavior Analysis",
        status = "success",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Behavior analysis content...")
      )
    )
    
    return(content)
  })
  
  # Macro analysis content
  output$macro_content <- renderUI({
    req(nav_state$secondary$macro)
    
    content <- switch(nav_state$secondary$macro,
      trends = bs4Card(
        title = "Trend Analysis",
        status = "warning",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Trend analysis content...")
      ),
      segments = bs4Card(
        title = "Market Segments",
        status = "danger",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Market segmentation content...")
      ),
      forecast = bs4Card(
        title = "Forecast Model",
        status = "secondary",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Forecast model content...")
      )
    )
    
    return(content)
  })
  
  # Settings content
  output$settings_content <- renderUI({
    req(nav_state$secondary$settings)
    
    content <- switch(nav_state$secondary$settings,
      profile = bs4Card(
        title = "Profile",
        status = "primary",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Profile settings...")
      ),
      preferences = bs4Card(
        title = "Preferences",
        status = "info",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Application preferences...")
      ),
      security = bs4Card(
        title = "Security Settings",
        status = "danger",
        solidHeader = TRUE,
        width = 12,
        p("Security settings...")
      )
    )
    
    return(content)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

# Section 6: Principle Relationships {#principle-relationships}

## Subsection 1: Extending Relationships {#extending-relationships}

This principle:

1. **Implements MP017 (Modularity Principle)** through modular navigation components
2. **Implements MP067 (UI Separation)** by separating navigation logic from content presentation
3. **Builds Upon R092 (bs4Dash Navbar Navigation)** using standard navigation components
4. **Related To UI_P009 (Single-Line UI Elements)** maintaining single-line layout for navigation elements
5. **Follows UI_P010 (Minimal CSS Usage)** using minimal necessary CSS
6. **Complements R014 (UI Hierarchy Rule)** providing specific hierarchical navigation implementation

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 11*  
*Related to: [MP017 Modularity Principle](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/meta_principles/MP017_modularity.qmd), [UI_P009 Single-Line UI Elements Principle](UI_P009_single_line_ui_elements.qmd)*
